payload.description: ASL interpretation via QR code for Deaf/hard-of-hearing customers

records.raw_text: Convo Access instantly connects people who are Deaf or hard-of-hearing with an American Sign Language interpreter to facilitate conversations with MTA staff. In the three months since the Pilot has launched, we have had over 200 successful calls made

description: Finalized early 2025. Includes simplifying, straightening, and consolidating routes, $30 million of new/increased service, new 'rush' limited routes. Set to go into effect June 2025. Did not include new SBS routes.
Reimagined bus network for Queens, driven by customer feedback, implementing Rush routes and modernized service across the borough

The Queens Bus Network Redesign aims to create a more efficient, reliable, and accessible bus system that better serves the needs of all riders, while prioritizing equity to address disparities in access and opportunity.

Proposed Final Plan for redesigning the Queens bus network, published Q4 2023. Proposes 121 total routes (91 local, 30 express) vs 113 existing routes. Includes bus priority expansion with NYC DOT, overnight network expansion, all-day frequent network expansion, and bus stop spacing changes.
Queens Bus Network Redesign Proposed Final Plan Addendum published December 2024
Board staff summary seeking approval to implement the Queens Bus Network Redesign as set forth in the December 2024 Proposed Final Plan Addendum for local and express bus service in Queens.

MTA's borough-wide plan to improve bus service in Queens, launched in 2019. Coordinating with NYC DOT on targeted corridors, including Grand Av/Grand St. Published Proposed Final Plan in December 2023.
Queens Bus Network Redesign New Draft Plan released in March 2022, one of the largest bus network redesigns in the country with over 100 routes
Targeting to finalize Queens Bus Network Redesign next year
Seeks to modernize the borough's bus network through a comprehensive redesign to deliver faster, more frequent, and more reliable service. Proposed Final Plan announced in December 2023.

Proposal to redesign the Queens bus network to better match ridership demands while improving service for customers. Simpler, more direct routes, improved bus stop balancing, enhanced connectivity, improved frequency on key routes, and expanded bus priority.
Borough-by-borough bus network redesign process; Proposed Final Plan for more robust bus service released in December 2023; public consultation process in March 2024

Queens Bus network redesign focused on simplifying and improving service for customers with more reliable service, faster travel, better connections, and improved ease of use

The Board approved the Queens Bus Network Redesign on February 26, 2025. Chair Lieber stated it would be funded by savings identified through the efficiency effort.

Transformative initiative launching in Phase 1 on June 29, 2025. 16 new routes will launch, 67 routes will be modified, 5 discontinued in Phase One. In Phase Two, 1 new route, 37 modified, 1 discontinued.
Historic effort to bring new and improved transit service to Queens, introducing rush routes and enhanced coverage. Phase 1 takes effect June 29, 2025.

Historic effort to bring new and improved transit service to Queens. Includes introduction of rush routes, enhanced coverage in Queens neighborhoods, and a promotional free transfer policy allowing Queens bus riders tapping with OMNY one additional free bus transfer within a three-hour period.
Launched Phase 1 in Eastern Queens; Phase 2 in Northwest and Southwest Queens starting August 31

One of the largest and most complex bus network redesigns in North American history, implemented in two phases during summer 2025. Introduced a modernized bus network for Queens. Six-year effort with three rounds of proposals, more than 18,000 comments, over 250 events.
Full rollout of a new bus network for Queens, launched in two phases over summer 2025, added 11 new routes, expanded overnight service, improved transit access for 68,000 more residents

Releasing a New Draft Plan for the Queens Bus Network Redesign in March 2022, based on over 11,000 comments received; virtual public workshops to be held spring and summer 2022

A reimagined plan, driven by customer feedback, to redesign the bus network in Queens with simpler routes, better connections, improved frequency, better bus stop spacing, and more busways and bus lanes
One of the largest bus network redesigns in the country, with over 100 routes, serving almost 800K average weekday riders. Draft plan released March 29, 2022
Queens Bus Network Redesign Project overview presented at the May 25, 2022 Board meeting by NYCT President Richard Davey and Dorian Statom, Director, Bus Service Planning
The Queens Bus Network Redesign team hosted 40 events over the past five months to gather feedback on the new draft plan. Final plan expected to be published in 2023.
The Queens Bus Network Redesign Team hosted 39 events to gather feedback, including 19 virtual events and 20 in-person.
project_name: Queens Bus Network Redesign Proposed Final Plan Addendum
Queens Bus Network Redesign Draft Plan
status: draft plan released March 2022, Proposed Final Plan pending
Proposed Final Plan released December 2023, public consultation process announced March 2024
key initiative in 2025 Final Proposed Budget
Proposed Final Plan to be put out to public

payload.description: Finalized early 2025. Includes simplifying, straightening, and consolidating routes, $30 million of new/increased service, new 'rush' limited routes. Set to go into effect June 2025. Did not include new SBS routes.

records.raw_text: On June 29, we will officially begin Phase 1 of the Queens Bus Network Redesign. This is a momentous occasion. It marks the culmination of six years of research, planning, and outreach.

records.display_name: QBNR Project has timeline event Phase 2 Launch
records.raw_text: The Customer Ambassador Program will return for the launch of Phase 2 on Sunday, August 31.

records.display_name: Jamaica Bus Terminal Relocation → 168th St Interim Terminal Opens
records.raw_text: the MTA officially relocated bus operations to the new interim 168th Street Bus Terminal in Jamaica, Queens, effective Sunday, June 1, 2025. This move temporarily replaces the 165th Street Bus Terminal, which has closed for redevelopment.

records.display_name: QBNR Project has timeline event Phase 1 Launch
records.raw_text: On June 29, we will officially begin Phase 1 of the Queens Bus Network Redesign.

records.display_name: 168th St Interim Terminal serves MTA and NICE bus routes

payload.description: 168th St Interim Terminal serves 10 MTA bus routes and 5 NICE bus routes, nearly 10,000 daily riders

records.raw_text: A total of 10 MTA bus routes and five Nassau Inter-County Express bus routes now operates from this interim terminal, serving nearly 10,000 daily riders.

records.display_name: Jamaica Bus Terminal moved from Merrick to 90th Avenue

records.raw_text: The terminal is moving from Merrick Boulevard to a new, state-of-the-art facility on 90th Avenue.

records.raw_text: The Bronx Bus Network Redesign is New York City Transit's first borough-wide bus network redesign conducted as part of the Fast Forward Plan

payload.description: F/M swap increases subway reliability, reduces delays, shortens travel times

records.raw_text: What does this mean for riders? Increased reliability, reduced delays, and shorter travel times.

payload.description: ACE program expanded to five new bus routes

records.raw_text: Last month, we expanded ACE to five new routes: the M2, M4, M42, M100, and Bx5.

records.display_name: Jamaica Bus Depot rebuild improves Queens bus service

payload.description: Jamaica Bus Depot rebuild supports modern bus fleet for Queens bus service improvement

records.raw_text: The MTA is rebuilding and expanding the Jamaica Bus Depot in Queens to better serve the community and support a modern, growing bus fleet. Once complete, the upgraded depot will handle the operation, maintenance, and storage of up to 300 buses. This will greatly improve bus service throughout Queens.
